name: agent-view-for-claude-code
description: Spawn, orchestrate, and monitor multiple Claude Code agents — each in its own git worktree — from one panel.

viewsWelcome.contents:
No Claude Code agents found yet.
[New Agent](command:mas.newAgent)
[Open Pinboard](command:mas.openCanvas)
[Configure Hooks](command:mas.configureHooks)
Agent View discovers running agents from ~/.claude/projects and can spawn new ones in isolated git worktrees. Open the Pinboard to watch agents on an infinite canvas, pin diffs, and hand work back.

mas.claudePath.description: Path to the Claude Code CLI executable.

mas.defaultModel.description: Model alias passed to spawned agents (e.g. 'opus', 'sonnet'). Empty = Claude Code default.

mas.worktreeRoot.description: Directory (relative to the repo root) where the extension creates per-agent git worktrees.

mas.hookPort.description: Local port the hook server listens on for live Claude Code events.

mas.spawnExtraFlags.description: Extra CLI flags appended to every spawned `claude` invocation.

mas.recentDays.description: Only discover sessions whose transcript was modified within this many days (the outer bound).

mas.recentHours.description: Display window: agents idle longer than this are hidden until you use 'Show Older Agents' (active agents always show). Bounded by mas.recentDays.

mas.conflictRadar.enabled.description: Conflict Radar — highlight files edited by more than one agent. Local-only; does NOT use your Claude subscription.

mas.attentionRouter.enabled.markdownDescription:
Attention Router — AI triage of which agent needs you, shown as a 'Needs you' inbox.

⚠️ Runs Claude Code in the background (`claude -p`) and **consumes your Claude subscription usage**. You'll be asked to confirm before the first run.

mas.mergeAdvisor.enabled.markdownDescription:
Merge Advisor — adds a command/toolbar button to rank multiple agents' diffs and recommend which to merge.

⚠️ **Consumes your Claude subscription usage** when run. You'll be asked to confirm before the first run.

mas.insights.triageModel.description: Model used for the Attention Router triage pass (cheap/fast model recommended).

mas.insights.mergeModel.description: Model used for the Merge Advisor ranking (quality model recommended).

mas.notifications.enabled.description: Show a notification when an agent needs your input, finishes, or hits an error.

mas.notifications.sound.description: Play a short system chime alongside notifications (best-effort; silently skipped if no player is available).

mas.notifications.onWaiting.description: Notify when an agent is waiting for your input (permission prompt / question).

mas.notifications.onDone.description: Notify when an Agent View-spawned agent finishes its task.

mas.notifications.onError.description: Notify when an agent hits an error.

mas.race.defaultCount.description: Default number of agents to spawn in an Agent Race.

mas.fanout.maxConcurrent.description: Maximum number of fan-out agents running at once. Remaining tasks queue and start as earlier agents finish.

mas.fanout.useWorktree.description: Run each fan-out agent in its own isolated git worktree (recommended). Disable to run them in the current directory.

mas.statusBar.enabled.description: Show a Fleet Pulse heartbeat in the status bar (e.g. "2 running · 1 needs you"), so you can monitor agents even when the Agent View panel is closed. It turns the warning color when an agent needs you; click it to jump there.

mas.review.allowLand.markdownDescription: Review & Land — allow the panel's **Squash-merge** action to commit an agent's branch into your current branch. Off by default; review, request-changes, copy-merge, and Open PR still work. The merge is refused on a dirty working tree, reversible until you push, and never deletes branches.

mas.review.maxDiffFiles.description: Maximum number of changed files to open as side-by-side diffs when you click an agent's Diff in Review & Land.

mas.review.ghPath.description: Path to the GitHub CLI (gh) used by Review & Land's Open PR. When gh isn't available, Open PR pushes the branch and copies a compare link instead.

mas.unattended.enabled.markdownDescription: Unattended Fleet — governed auto-pilot for Agent View-spawned agents. New agents auto-accept edits (`--permission-mode acceptEdits`; **Bash still prompts**), agents that stall mid-plan get nudged to continue, and an estimated cost meter shows in Fleet Pulse. Off by default.

mas.unattended.autoAcceptEdits.description: While Unattended Fleet is on, spawn agents with --permission-mode acceptEdits so file edits don't block. Bash and other tools still prompt (and escalate to a notification). Turn off to keep full prompting.

mas.unattended.nudgeStuckAfterSeconds.description: How long a managed agent may sit idle mid-plan before Unattended Fleet nudges it to continue.

mas.unattended.maxNudges.description: Maximum consecutive nudges before Unattended Fleet stops and escalates a stuck agent to you.

mas.unattended.maxCostUsd.description: Per-agent estimated-cost cap in USD. When a managed agent's estimated token cost passes this, Unattended Fleet pauses it (stops the run; its branch and diff are kept). 0 disables the cap.

mas.unattended.pricing.markdownDescription: Override the per-million-token prices used for the cost estimate. Keys `opus` / `sonnet` / `haiku`, each `{ input, output, cacheRead, cacheWrite }` in USD per 1M tokens. Defaults: Opus $5/$25, Sonnet $3/$15, Haiku $1/$5 (cache read ~0.1x input, write ~1.25x input). Estimates only.

scripts.build: npm run build:webview && npm run build:ext
scripts.build:webview: cd webview-ui && npm install --no-audit --no-fund && npm run build
